question_text: During aerobic respiration, glucose is broken down to release energy. Which statement correctly describes what happens to the energy released and how it relates to food energy content?

options.label: A
options.text: The energy released from breaking chemical bonds in glucose is used for movement and heat, and equals the energy content labeled on food packets

options.label: B
options.text: The energy released is lost as carbon dioxide and water, so food energy cannot be used by the body

options.label: C
options.text: The energy is stored permanently in muscles and never used unless the person exercises

options.label: D
options.text: The energy released is only used for digestion, not for other body activities

explanation: When glucose undergoes aerobic respiration (C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂ → 6CO₂ + 6H₂O + energy), the energy from breaking chemical bonds is released and used immediately for muscle contraction, maintaining body temperature, and other processes—this is the energy content shown on food packets.
